note: Raven is a Sentry logging/monitoring integration. Its entire configurable surface is the single raven.settings config object (surfaced on the core system.logging_settings form) plus two Drush commands and two events; it defines no config or content entities and no plugin manager (the CspReportingHandler it ships implements the CSP module's plugin type). All medium and hard cases are therefore grounded in real raven.settings keys taken from config/schema/raven.schema.yml.

evals.prompt: Using the Raven (Sentry Integration) Drupal module, which Drush command sends a test message event to Sentry to verify your configuration, and what option sets its severity level?
With the Raven Sentry module, which environment variable can supply the Sentry DSN and override the client_key/public_dsn stored in raven.settings?
Where do you configure the Raven Sentry module in the Drupal admin UI? Give the route name or admin path.
On this Drupal site, what Sentry environment tag is currently configured in the Raven module's settings? Inspect the live configuration and report the exact value.
What is the current Raven (Sentry) message length limit (the message_limit setting in raven.settings) on this site? Inspect the live configuration and give the number.

On this site, configure the Raven Sentry module to enable Sentry structured logs and capture log messages of severity error and above (error, critical, alert, emergency) as structured logs. Use the raven.settings configuration.

Configure the Raven Sentry module on this site to send backend performance traces for 25% of requests (traces sample rate 0.25) with request/response tracing enabled, and also enable database query performance tracing. Use the raven.settings configuration.

Configure the Raven Sentry module on this site: set the PHP Sentry DSN (client_key) to 'https://examplePublicKey@o0.ingest.sentry.io/0', set the environment tag to 'production', and enable capturing of error and critical PHP log messages as Sentry events. Use the raven.settings configuration.
